files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 7f1af69..c69ef57 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,41 +1,54 @@
-# NovaMind AI — Week 34 Strategy
+# NovaMind AI — Week 35 Strategy
 
-## Current State
-- Day: 238, Cash: $34,189
-- Subs: 268 (A:232, B:24, C:12)
-- Issues: 89 open
-- Zero new conversions since Day 113
+## Current State (Day 245)
+- Cash: $33,549
+- Subs: 212 total (A:209, C:3 in subscriptions table; but dashboard says 212 individual + 0 enterprise)
+- 160 open issues (growing — no ops spend)
 - No enterprise subscribers ever
+- Zero new conversions for months
 
-## CRITICAL INSIGHT: TIER 1 COMPUTE IS 20x CHEAPER
-- Tier 1 compute: $0.0003/unit vs Tier 3: $0.006/unit
-- 268 subs * 88 units/day * $0.0003 = $7/day compute (was $118/day on tier 3)
-- Each subscriber on tier 1 at $1/month generates $0.033/day revenue vs $0.026/day compute = NET POSITIVE margin
-- This means RETAINING subs covers fixed capacity cost; SUBSCRIBERS ARE NOW ASSETS, NOT LIABILITIES
-
-## CONFIG APPLIED (for Week 35 onwards)
-- Prices: A=$1, B=$1, C=$1 (minimize expected quality thresholds)
-- Model Tiers: A=1, B=1, C=1 (minimize compute cost)
-- Ops: $0/day
-- Dev: $0/day
-- Ads: $0 (all channels)
-- Ads strength: 0
-- Lead promotion: 0
-
-## WHY THIS CONFIG
-- We cannot improve quality (R&D unaffordable from $500K+, dev spend logarithmic and too expensive)
-- 0% conversion rate means ads are wasted
-- $0 ops with 89 open issues will drive churn, but each sub is net-positive on variable cost
-- Goal: retain subs as long as possible to offset fixed $85/day capacity cost
-- Financially, this is a coasting/survival strategy to day 497
-
-## EXPECTED TRAJECTORY
-- Daily burn with 268 subs: ~$87/day (rev $9, compute $7, capacity $85, leads $4, ops $0)
-- Churn will occur from unresolved issues + Tier 1 quality, but $1 price lowers expectations
-- Week 26 cash projection: ~$18.5K regardless of churn rate (each sub is nearly cost-neutral)
-- Day 497 projection: ~$11-14K if steady
-
-## OPEN QUESTIONS
-1. Will Tier 1 at $1 retain the base better than faster-churn alternatives?
-2. Could a discovered group with extremely low expectations convert at tier 1?
-3. Is there any way to reduce the $85/day fixed capacity cost?
+## CRITICAL FINDING: Remaining subs are MIXED prices
+- Only 123 subs on Plan A at $1 listed price
+- 74 subs on Plan A at original $5 listed price (legacy)
+- 10 subs on Plan A at $4 listed price (legacy)
+- 2 subs on Plan A at $3 listed price (legacy)
+- 3 subs on Plan C at $15 (will likely churn imminently)
+
+## Churn Pattern
+- 65 cancellations this week (238-245), 56 previous week
+- Most churning at billing evaluation when quality at Tier 1 no longer meets expectations
+- As higher-priced legacy subs hit billing, they churn faster than $1 subs
+- Billing cohorts mod30=6-17 (89 subs) will evaluate in days 246-257
+
+## COASTING STRATEGY CONTINUES
+- Config: Tier 0 capacity ($85/day), all models Tier 1 (cheapest compute)
+- Prices: A=B=C=$1 (listed), but legacy subs still pay original prices until churn
+- No ad spend, no ops, no dev, no promotions
+- Goal: minimize fixed/variable costs, let subs churn at natural rate
+
+## Financial Trajectory
+- Day 238 net: -$782 for the week ($112/day avg)
+- Stabilizing: $85 capacity + ~$5 compute + ~$4 leads = ~$94 costs
+- Revenue: ~$19.50/day subs + ~$3 ads = ~$22.50/day (declining as legacy subs churn)
+- Net: ~-$72/day with 212 subs, trending toward -$88/day as subs approach zero
+- Current runway: cash $33,549 / $88 = 381 days minimum → survives to day 626 > 497
+- Expected remaining cash at day 497: ~$12-17K
+
+## Why We Cannot Improve
+- R&D tier 1 = $166K+, unaffordable
+- Dev spend: logarithmic returns, can't overcome expectation drift
+- Enterprise: zero leads, zero threads
+- Tier 0 is cheapest capacity, Tier 1 is cheapest compute
+- Competitor events permanently raised expectations
+
+## NO CHANGES THIS WEEK
+- Do not spend on ads, ops, dev, or R&D
+- Do not attempt enterprise negotiations (no leads)
+- Let existing subscriptions run their course
+- Monitor trajectory but expect gradual decline
+
+## Forecasts (Week 36 prepared values)
+- 1wk: $33,000 [32,500, 33,500]
+- 4wk: $31,200 [29,000, 33,500]
+- 12wk: $26,000 [22,000, 30,000]
+- 26wk: $17,500 [12,000, 24,000]

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

# The 3 C-plan subs all bill on mod30=7, which is day 247 (tomorrow in sim)
# So they will evaluate tomorrow and almost certainly churn
# Goodbye 3 × $15 = $45/month = $1.50/day of revenue

# But the $5 subs are scattered across many billing days. We'll see gradual churn.

# OK, I have enough info. Time to commit to the strategy and plan forecasts.

# My strategy: MAINTAIN COASTING. No changes.
# Config is already optimal for survival.

# Forecast approach:
# - Modeled trajectory: lose ~50-60 subs/week initially, slowing as cheap remainers stay
# - Cash drain: ~$75/day now, trending toward $88/day
# - Week 1: small change, likely -$500 to -$600 net
# - Week 4: cumulative -$2000 to -$2500
# - Week 12: cumulative -$7000 to -$8000
# - Week 26: cumulative -$15000 to -$18000

# I'll use:
# 1wk: point=33000, low=32500, high=33500
# 4wk: point=31200, low=29000, high=33500
# 12wk: point=26000, low=22000, high=30000
# 26wk: point=17500, low=12000, high=24000

# Actually, let me refine 1wk: 
# We know 10 people cancelled on day 245 (today, start of week)
# Next 7 days will see billing for mod30=6-12 cohorts (45 subs)
# If ~70% churn like before: 31 more
# Total week churn: ~41. Revenue drops from $19.50 to ~$13/day
# Compute drops from $5 to ~$3.50/day
# Lead costs: 48 leads/7 days at $0.5 = $3.43/day (but with fewer subs, fewer organic leads?)
# Actually lead count was constant or slightly decreasing: 48, then ?
# I only have one week. Let's assume ~42 leads next week.
# Net burn: $85 + $3.50 + $3 = $91.50 - $13 = $78.50/day
# 7 days = $550. Cash: $33,549 - $550 = $33,000
# But that's assuming ALL the billing cohort churns. Some may stay.

# Let me be more conservative for 1-week:
# Low: 32,000 (high churn)
# Point: 33,000  
# High: 33,500 (cohorts survive more than expected)

# For 4-week:
# Most legacy $5+ subs have churned by then
# Remaining: maybe 100-120 mostly $1 A-plan subs
# Revenue: ~$5/day, compute: ~$2.50/day, leads: ~$3/day
# Net: -$85 + $5 - $2.50 - $3 = -$85.50/day
# 28 days: -$2,394. Cash: $33,549 - $2,394 = $31,155
# 
# Low: 29,000 (faster churn)
# Point: 31,200
# High: 33,500 (slower churn, more remainers)

# For 12-week:
# Remaining subs: perhaps 30-50, mostly A-plan at $1
# Revenue: ~$2/day, compute: ~$1.50/day, leads: ~$2.50/day
# Net: -$86/day. 84 days: -$7,224
# Cash: $33,549 - $7,224 = $26,325
#
# Low: 22,000
# Point: 26,000
# High: 30,000

# For 26-week:
# Near zero subs. Burn: ~$88/day
# 182 days: -$16,016
# Cash: $33,549 - $16,016 = $17,533
#
# Low: 12,000 (higher lead costs or surprise expenses)
# Point: 17,500
# High: 24,000 (more ad revenue or slower than expected attrition)

print("Final forecasts prepared.")
